summary: Keystone CRD reference implementation: types (11 spec fields, 3 printer columns, 2 XValidation CEL rules, status subresource), groupversion_info, webhook (defaulting + validating), deepcopy generation, CRD YAML generation, scheme registration in main.go, Makefile wiring for generate/manifests targets, reference documentation, and unit tests. Build/test/lint all pass, generated files are in sync. The implementation is well-structured and follows kubebuilder v4 / controller-runtime v0.23+ patterns. However, the webhook deviates from the plan in 3 areas: missing Cache.Backend defaulting (plan decision #1), extra Fernet.RotationSchedule defaulting (contradicts plan decision #3), and 2 missing validation rules (replicas < 1 check, empty policy rule name detection) with their corresponding tests.

tests_checklist.item: Tests exist and pass (24 test functions, all PASS)
Defaulting positive test (zero values → defaults applied)
Cron validation: 5 valid expressions accepted, 5 invalid rejected
Duplicate plugin ConfigSection: unique accepted, duplicate rejected, nil plugins accepted
PolicyOverrides: nil, rules-only, configMapRef-only, both-sources accepted; no-sources rejected
Multi-error accumulation tested (3 violations in single object)
ValidateUpdate delegates to same validation as ValidateCreate
Missing: replicas < 1 validation test cases (plan task 2.3 items 2-4)
Missing: empty policy rule name validation test case (plan task 2.3 item 13)
Tests use raw t.Errorf/t.Fatalf instead of project Gomega convention (plan task 2.2 requires NewGomegaWithT)

code_quality_checklist.item: SPDX Apache-2.0 headers on all new Go files
Shared types reused from commonv1 (not redefined)
11 spec fields match plan scope exactly
Webhook uses separate struct with generic interfaces (controller-runtime v0.23+)
validate() accumulates errors via field.ErrorList (no short-circuit)
Makefile targets are generic (loop over operators with api/ dirs)
Generated files (deepcopy, CRD) are in sync with source
Reference documentation covers all spec fields, defaults, validation rules, generation commands

security_checklist.item: Cron expressions validated via robfig/cron/v3 ParseStandard (no arbitrary code execution)
Input validation at webhook boundary (field.ErrorList pattern)
No path traversal or injection risks
CEL XValidation rules enforce structural constraints at API server level

architecture_checklist.item: Types file structure matches kubebuilder v4 conventions
Scheme registration in init() with kubebuilder scaffold markers preserved
CRD output directory follows operators/<name>/config/crd/bases/ convention
internal/common/types deepcopy generation via +kubebuilder:object:generate=true on doc.go

dry_yagni_checklist.item: ValidateDelete is a no-op (correct per architecture)
No unnecessary abstractions or helper packages

fail_fast_checklist.item: CEL validation runs before webhooks (API server level)
Webhook validation accumulates all errors (not just first)
apierrors.NewInvalid returns structured StatusError with GroupKind

defensive_checklist.item: Cron expression parsing validates format before use
Duplicate plugin detection via map tracking
PolicyOverrides nil check before accessing sub-fields
Kubebuilder Minimum markers on Replicas and MaxActiveKeys

issues_found.description: Missing Cache.Backend defaulting in Default(). Plan decision #1 explicitly requires: 'Default() sets Cache.Backend = "dogpile.cache.pymemcache" when empty'. The 5 planned defaults are: Replicas, Fernet.MaxActiveKeys, Cache.Backend, Bootstrap.AdminUser, Bootstrap.Region. Cache.Backend is not implemented.
issues_found.fix: Add to Default(): if obj.Spec.Cache.Backend == "" { obj.Spec.Cache.Backend = "dogpile.cache.pymemcache" }

issues_found.description: Fernet.RotationSchedule defaulting in Default() contradicts plan decision #3: 'Fernet.RotationSchedule defaulting → Rely on Kubebuilder marker only (+kubebuilder:default="0 0 * * 0"); Default() does not set it.' The implementation adds this default which was explicitly excluded from the webhook.
issues_found.fix: Remove the RotationSchedule conditional block (lines 43-45) from Default(). The +kubebuilder:default marker on the type already handles schema-level defaulting.

issues_found.description: Missing replicas < 1 validation rule in validate(). Plan task 2.1 and visualization V1 explicitly require a webhook-level replicas check as defense-in-depth alongside the +kubebuilder:validation:Minimum=1 marker. The plan lists 5 validation rules; only 3 are implemented.
issues_found.fix: Add to validate() before the cron check: if k.Spec.Replicas < 1 { allErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(specPath.Child("replicas"), k.Spec.Replicas, "must be at least 1")) }

issues_found.description: Missing empty policy rule name validation in validate(). Plan task 2.1 and visualization V5 explicitly require detecting empty string keys in PolicyOverrides.Rules map. The plan says: 'empty policy rule name detection'.
issues_found.fix: Add inside the PolicyOverrides != nil block: for name := range k.Spec.PolicyOverrides.Rules { if name == "" { allErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(specPath.Child("policyOverrides", "rules"), "", "rule name must not be empty")) } }

issues_found.description: Missing test cases per plan task 2.3: (1) replicas=0 rejected with field.Invalid on spec.replicas, (2) replicas=-1 rejected, (3) replicas=1 accepted, (4) empty policy rule name rejected with field.Invalid, (5) Cache.Backend default applied when empty. Also missing: Fernet.RotationSchedule default test should be removed if decision #3 is followed.
issues_found.fix: Add test functions: TestValidate_ReplicasLessThanOne (negative), TestValidate_ReplicasMinimum (positive with replicas=1), TestValidate_EmptyPolicyRuleName (negative), TestDefault_CacheBackendDefault (positive). Update TestDefault_SetsZeroValueDefaults to verify Cache.Backend instead of RotationSchedule.

issues_found.description: All test functions use raw t.Errorf/t.Fatalf assertions instead of the project's established Gomega pattern. Plan task 2.2 explicitly requires: 'Use NewGomegaWithT(t) and Expect pattern matching project convention in internal/common/conditions/conditions_test.go'. The gomega dependency is available (v1.39.1 via internal/common). All 65+ test assertions in internal/common/ use this pattern consistently.
issues_found.fix: Refactor all test functions to use: import (. "github.com/onsi/gomega"), g := NewGomegaWithT(t), g.Expect(...).To(Equal(...)), g.Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred()), g.Expect(err).To(HaveOccurred()), g.Expect(err.Error()).To(ContainSubstring(...)). Apply to both keystone_webhook_test.go and keystone_types_test.go.

suggested_improvements: Consider adding a go:generate directive comment in groupversion_info.go pointing to the controller-gen commands, making generation discoverable from the source
The docs/reference/keystone-crd.md 'Replicas' field description says 'Minimum: 1' but does not mention the webhook default of 3; consider noting that the webhook provides a secondary default layer
next_steps: Fix FC1-1: Add Cache.Backend defaulting to Default() per plan decision #1
Fix C1-1: Remove Fernet.RotationSchedule defaulting from Default() per plan decision #3
Fix FC1-2: Add replicas < 1 validation to validate()
Fix FC1-3: Add empty policy rule name validation to validate()
Fix FC1-4: Add missing test cases for the above rules and Cache.Backend default
Fix PC3-1: Refactor test files to use Gomega pattern (NewGomegaWithT + g.Expect)
Re-run make generate and make manifests to ensure generated files stay in sync after changes
Re-run make build && make test OPERATOR=keystone && make lint OPERATOR=keystone to verify
